content: Do NATO GDP metrics show contributions or obscure them?

If the US at ~3.4% GDP and the UK at ~2.3% are treated as similar burden-sharers by that metric, pick one: does the metric overstate UK effort, understate US effort, or both? Defend your choice with one concrete capability delta (e.g., carrier/blue-water + space command vs. UK Trident renewal).

Name one capability the US fields at ~3.4% that the UK cannot field at ~2.3%, and explain in one sentence how the GDP % masks that gap. No new metrics, no “it depends” — pick the stronger direction of bias and justify it.
